data.title: European markets look for direction as uncertainty over trade lingers
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 7.00am ET

London (UKX) +0.04% to 8,607. 

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.47% to 23,527. Germany's inflation drops to 2.1% in April

France (CAC:IND) -0.59% to 7,827.

Spain's inflation was recorded at 2.2% in April.

Slovakia’s annual inflation rate slowed to 3.7% in April.

Hungary's construction output shrank by 3.4% Y/Y in March.

Sweden’s annual inflation rate eased to 0.3% in April.

The annual inflation rate in Finland held steady at 0.5% in April.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 0.12% lower to approximately 544.5, fluctuating around the flat line. The markets have shown signs of losing steam, as investor sentiment turned cautious amid uncertainty over the specifics and enforcement of global trade agreements. On the corporate front, Burberry Group (OTCPK:BURBY) jumped following the announcement of several organizational changes.

Coming up in the next session: A lighter day on the European economic calendar today.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield down 5 basis points at 4.45%.

Germany's 10-year yield was dwn 1 basis point to 2.67%.

UK's 10-year yield was flat at 4.67%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Nexxen International Non-GAAP EPS of $0.16 beats by $0.11, revenue of $78.33M beats by $5.32M
data.description:
• Nexxen International Ltd. press release (NASDAQ:NEXN): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.16 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $78.33M (+5.2% Y/Y) beats by $5.32M.
  • Record Q1 Contribution ex-TAC of $75.0 million, up 8% year-over-year
  • Record Q1 programmatic revenue of $71.8 million, up 10% year-over-year
  • Record Q1 CTV revenue of $26.4 million, up 40% year-over-year
  • Nexxen reaffirms its prior full year 2025 financial guidance:
    • Full year 2025 Contribution ex-TAC of approximately $380 million
    • Full year 2025 programmatic re
    • venue to reflect approximately 90% of full year 2025 revenue
    • Full year 2025 Adjusted EBITDA of approximately $125 million

data.title: Immuno-oncology company Affimed to be delisted from Nasdaq after insolvency filing
data.description:
• Clinical-stage immuno-oncology company Affimed N.V (NASDAQ:AFMD) received a written notice from the staff of the Nasdaq notifying the company that, in accordance with Nasdaq listing rule.
  • The company’s shares will be suspended at the opening of business on May 20, 2025, and the company filed with the U.S. SEC to delist the company’s securities from The Nasdaq Stock Market.
  • As previously reported, on May 13, 2025, the company filed for insolvency with the local court of Mannheim in Germany.
  • The stock price slumped 10% to $0.12 on Wednesday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: Novo Nordisk in pact with Septerna for oral obesity drugs
data.description:
Septerna (NASDAQ:SEPN) climbed ~63% in the premarket on Wednesday after the Wegovy maker Novo Nordisk (NVO) agreed to jointly develop and commercialize oral small-molecule drugs for obesity and other cardiometabolic diseases with the company in a deal worth up to $2.2B.  

Septerna, a clinical-stage biopharma company focused on developing treatments targeted at a group of cell membrane receptors called G protein-coupled receptors (GPCR), including GLP-1s, is set to receive over $200M in upfront and near-term milestone payments from NVO as part of the deal.

The total payments to the company from the Danish biotech can hit $2.2B, including the upfront payments as well as research, development, and commercial milestone payments. Additionally, San Francisco, California-based Septerna (NASDAQ:SEPN) will be entitled to tiered royalties on global net sales of marketed products.

Under the deal terms, Novo (NVO) will bear all R&D expenses for partnered programs, which will initially comprise of four development programs directed at GPCR targets, including GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors, the companies said. The agreement is expected to close in Q2 2025.

data.title: Verve Therapeutics GAAP EPS of -$0.35 beats by $0.32, revenue of $32.98M

• Verve Therapeutics press release (NASDAQ:VERV): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.35 beats by $0.32.

data.title: Endava Non-GAAP EPS of £0.34, revenue of £194.8M; initiates Q4 outlook
data.description:
• Endava press release (NYSE:DAVA): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of £0.34.
  • Revenue of £194.8M (+11.7% Y/Y).
  • Revenue increase at constant currency (a non-IFRS measure)* was 12.4% for Q3 FY2025.
  • Net cash from operating activities was £18.7 million in Q3 FY2025, compared to net cash from operating activities of £3.0 million in the same period in the prior year.
  • Adjusted free cash flow (a non-IFRS measure)* was £17.5 million in Q3 FY2025, compared to £2.2 million in the same period in the prior year.
  • At March 31, 2025, Endava had cash and cash equivalents of £68.3 million, compared to £62.4 million at June 30, 2024.
  • Number of clients with over £1 million in revenue on a rolling twelve-month basis was 136 at March 31, 2025, compared to 142 clients at March 31, 2024.
  • Top 10 clients accounted for 39% of revenue in Q3 FY2025, compared to 34% in the same period in the prior year.

  • OUTLOOK:

    Fourth Quarter Fiscal Year 2025:

    Endava expects revenue will be in the range of £186.0 million to £188.0 million, representing a constant currency revenue change of between (1.0)% and 0.0% on a year over year basis. Endava expects adjusted diluted EPS to be in the range of £0.22 to £0.24 per share.

    Full Fiscal Year 2025:

    Endava expects revenue will be in the range of £771.5 million to £773.5 million, representing a constant currency revenue increase of between 6.0% and 6.5% on a year over year basis. Endava expects adjusted diluted EPS to be in the range of £1.11 to £1.13 per share.

data.title: Franklin Street Properties to review options, including a possible sale
data.description:
Franklin Street Properties (NYSE:FSP) stock rose 5.5% in Wednesday premarket trading after the company's board started a review of the office REIT's strategic alternatives for ways to maximize shareholder value. The review will explore such options as the sale of assets, the sale of the company, and refinancing existing debt, it said on Wednesday.

"We believe that FSP's share price does not adequately reflect the underlying value of our real estate, and, accordingly, we have undertaken this strategic review process to explore opportunities to eliminate this disconnect," said Chairman and CEO George J. Carter.

In the past year, Franklin Street (NYSE:FSP) shares have dropped 24%, compared with the S&P 500's 13% increase.

The company has hired BofA Securities as its financial adviser in connection with the review. Franklin Street (FSP) gave no assurances regarding the outcome or a timetable for completion of the review process.

data.title: ARS Pharmaceuticals GAAP EPS of -$0.35 in-line, revenue of $7.97M beats by $0.49M
data.description:
• ARS Pharmaceuticals press release (NASDAQ:SPRY): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.35 in-line.
